Trigger: The two gladiators are on the sand but not yet swinging
Condition/Variant: Fires once, before start_fighting() is called
Context: The held breath before the fight. Both fighters are posed and idle-bobbing, the crowd is settled, and the player has a beat to read the nameplates and see what their gladiator is wearing. Everything after this is chaos, so this moment should feel deliberately still.
SFX desc: Announcer horn or gate clank, single hit, room for the crowd to swell under it
VFX/Anim: Wanted: brief camera settle or gate open; nothing exists today
Notes: No pre-fight beat exists; _ready() starts the fight immediately. Adding this means inserting a wait before start_fighting().

Trigger: Both gladiators begin their attack rhythms
Condition/Variant: Always, once per match
Context: The fight is on. Both fighters snap out of idle and the first swings land within a second. This is the go signal and it needs to be unmistakable, because the player only has from here until someone dies to feed items in.
SFX desc: Sharp start-of-round hit - gong, whistle or blade-scrape - short so the first swing is not buried
VFX/Anim: Existing: units switch from idle_bob into their attack cadence
Notes: Has its own signal as of the SFX wiring pass. level_started is still emitted alongside it, but level_01.gd:_ready emits that one too, so the fight's go-signal audio hangs off match_started instead.

Trigger: The first damage of the match is dealt
Condition/Variant: First unit_hurt emission only; suppressed for every hit after
Context: First blood. Mechanically identical to any other hit, but it is the moment the match stops being a staredown. Worth a one-time crowd lift so the opening reads as an escalation rather than a flat loop of swings.
SFX desc: Crowd surge or single stinger layered over the normal hit sound
VFX/Anim: Reuse the standard hit flash and shake
UI response: First damage number of the match
Notes: Needs a one-shot latch in the battle controller; unit_hurt fires on every hit.

Trigger: The enemy gladiator dies and the fight stops
Condition/Variant: battle_ended(hero_won = true)
Context: The player's gladiator is the one left standing. This lands the instant the enemy hits zero, roughly a second before the victory panel appears, so it is the emotional payoff and the panel is just the paperwork.
SFX desc: Triumphant stinger, crowd roar tail; must sit above the ambient bed
VFX/Anim: Existing: loser plays the death animation; winner returns to idle
Notes: battle_ended carries a bool, so SoundEffectConnector can bind it, but one connector cannot tell victory from defeat - it plays on both. Two rows means either a code hook or two argument-aware listeners.

Trigger: The hero gladiator dies and the fight stops
Condition/Variant: battle_ended(hero_won = false)
Context: The player's fighter goes down. They almost certainly saw it coming - the health bar has been draining and they were scrambling for meat - so this should feel like a confirmation of a slow loss, not a jump scare.
SFX desc: Deflating stinger, crowd falls away rather than roars
VFX/Anim: Existing: hero plays the death animation
Notes: See the victory row: the same signal covers both outcomes.

Trigger: The victory/defeat panel fades in
Condition/Variant: After BattleEndOverlay.death_anim_wait (0.8s) has elapsed
Context: The panel arrives once the death animation has played out. By now the outcome sound has already told the player what happened, so this is a quieter UI beat that hands control back and points at the restart button.
SFX desc: Soft panel whoosh, deliberately understated
VFX/Anim: Existing: OverlayRoot becomes visible
UI response: Result label and restart button appear
Notes: The reveal can be cancelled mid-wait by hide_result() or a scene teardown - do not start a loop here.

Trigger: The player presses Restart on the result panel
Condition/Variant: Always
Context: The player wants another go. The whole scene reloads immediately after, so anything here is cut off almost at once - keep it to a confirmation click.
SFX desc: Standard UI confirm click
VFX/Anim: Existing: scene reloads
Notes: reload_current_scene() fires on the same frame; a long sample will be truncated.

Trigger: A gladiator starts an attack on a pattern beat
Condition/Variant: A WeaponDef occupies the RIGHT_HAND slot
Context: A sword swing. The blade is visibly in the fighter's hand and this swing will hit for weapon damage instead of a bare fist, so it should sound heavier and more dangerous than the unarmed version - the player equipped that sword and should hear the difference every time.
SFX desc: Blade whoosh, metallic edge, weighted
VFX/Anim: Existing: attack animation restarted from frame 0
Notes: The moment is implemented but the armed/unarmed split is not exposed: unit_attacked carries no weapon info and roll_attack_damage only runs later at the strike. Needs the beat to query equipment.has_slot(RIGHT_HAND). Also note unit_attacked has two arguments, so SoundEffectConnector cannot bind it as written.

Trigger: A gladiator starts an attack on a pattern beat
Condition/Variant: No weapon in RIGHT_HAND (no equipment component, or the sword broke)
Context: A bare-handed swing. This is the default state and also what a fighter falls back to the moment their sword shatters, so it should sound thinner and less capable - the audible reminder that they need a weapon.
SFX desc: Cloth and air, dull, no metal
VFX/Anim: Existing: attack animation restarted from frame 0
Notes: Same split as the armed row. Every enemy is permanently in this state - enemies have no EquipmentComponent by design.

Trigger: A gladiator swings while out of stamina
Condition/Variant: stamina_component.has_stamina() is false, so _speed_scale() returns 0.5
Context: A tired swing. The whole attack rhythm is running at half speed and the fighter is visibly slower, so this variant should sound laboured - the cue that a stamina potion is overdue.
SFX desc: Slower, breathier version of the normal swing, with an effort grunt
VFX/Anim: Existing: the entire pattern playhead runs at half rate
UI response: Stamina bar is empty on the nameplate
Notes: Layer or substitute against the armed/unarmed swing rather than adding a third exclusive sound.

Trigger: The attack animation reaches its lunge apex and applies damage
Condition/Variant: Attacker and target both still alive at the apex
Context: The connection itself, about a quarter second after the swing starts. This is the impact frame the whole animation builds to, and it is where the impact sound belongs - the swing sound sold the wind-up, this sells the hit.
SFX desc: Impact transient; the hit-specific rows below layer on top
VFX/Anim: Existing: target flashes white and shakes
UI response: Damage number spawns above the target
Notes: Called by the attack animation's method track, not by code - see the AnimationPlayer 'attack' track. Fires before take_damage, so the armored/unarmored variant is not yet known here.

Trigger: A swing reaches its strike frame with nothing left to hit
Condition/Variant: Target already dead (or null) when _on_attack_strike runs
Context: The fighter follows through on a swing at an opponent who is already down. Rare, and it happens on the final blow of a match, so it should read as a wasted effort rather than an error.
SFX desc: Swing tail with no impact - the absence of the hit is the point
VFX/Anim: Existing: animation plays out fully with no damage applied
Notes: Currently a silent early-return. Mostly visible at match end when both fighters have swings in flight.

Trigger: A gladiator takes damage with nothing to soak it
Condition/Variant: No EquipmentComponent, or no armor pieces equipped - full damage lands
Context: Bare flesh and bone. The gladiator has no helmet or breastplate and the blow lands on them directly. This should sting, and it must read as clearly worse than the armored version so the player feels the cost of leaving their fighter bare.
SFX desc: Wet, fleshy thud with a body reaction; the most painful hit in the set
VFX/Anim: Existing: white flash (0.08s) and a four-step shake
UI response: Damage number, nameplate health bar drops
Notes: Both enemies are always in this state. unit_hurt carries two arguments, so SoundEffectConnector cannot bind it directly.

Trigger: A gladiator takes damage that armor partially soaks
Condition/Variant: At least one ArmorDef equipped and the mitigated amount is still above zero
Context: The blow gets through, but not all of it. The armor is doing its job and quietly wearing down for it. This should sit between the bare hit and the fully-blocked one, so the player can hear their armor working without being told it saved them completely.
SFX desc: Metal-over-body: a clang with a soft impact under it
VFX/Anim: Existing: white flash and shake, same as unarmored
UI response: Reduced damage number, health bar drops less
Notes: Mitigated damage is what every listener receives, so the damage number already tells the truth. Exactly one random armor piece takes 1 wear on this hit - see equipment_armor_wear.

Trigger: A gladiator takes a hit that armor cancels entirely
Condition/Variant: Mitigated damage clamps to 0
Context: The armor ate the whole blow. A 0 still floats up and the fighter still flinches, so this is the sound of a hit that did not hurt - satisfying, dull, no pain. It also means a durability point just burned, so it should carry a hint of wear.
SFX desc: Solid deadened clang, no flesh, slight scrape of wear underneath
VFX/Anim: Existing: still flashes and shakes - deliberate honest feedback
UI response: A literal 0 damage number, health bar unchanged
Notes: The code comment at take_damage explains the intent: a fully absorbed hit still shows a 0 so the player knows the armor ate it and wore down for it.

Trigger: The incoming blow came from a weapon
Condition/Variant: Attacker had a WeaponDef equipped when the strike landed
Context: Attacker-side flavour on the impact. A sword hit and a fist hit should not sound the same on the receiving end either - this is the layer that says what hit you, while the armored/unarmored rows say what it hit.
SFX desc: Edged component layered onto the base impact
VFX/Anim: Reuse the standard flash and shake
Notes: Layer, do not replace. The receiving side currently has no idea what struck it.

Trigger: The incoming blow came from a bare hand
Condition/Variant: Attacker had no weapon equipped
Context: The unarmed counterpart of the row above. Should sound noticeably weaker so a player watching an enemy punch their armored gladiator understands they are in no danger.
SFX desc: Blunt low thump layered onto the base impact
VFX/Anim: Reuse the standard flash and shake
Notes: Layer, do not replace.

Trigger: A gladiator's health crosses a low threshold
Condition/Variant: Health falls below a set fraction of max; fires once per crossing
Context: The fighter is in real danger. For the hero this is the cue to spend meat right now; for the enemy it is the cue that the match is nearly won. It must be readable without looking at the nameplate, because the player's eyes are on the item pile.
SFX desc: Heartbeat or strained breath; consider a slow loop rather than a one-shot
VFX/Anim: Wanted: red vignette or a persistent tint on the fighter
Notes: Must latch, or it re-fires on every subsequent hit. Decide whether it re-arms when health is healed back above the threshold.

Trigger: The hero gladiator's health reaches zero
Condition/Variant: HealthComponent.died on the hero
Context: The player's fighter collapses. The death animation rotates and fades the sprite over the next moment, and the defeat panel is already on a timer. This is the loss, felt at the body rather than in the UI.
SFX desc: Body fall with a final breath; heavier than the enemy version
VFX/Anim: Existing: 'death' animation - sprite rotates and self_modulate fades
Notes: unit_died carries the unit node; a listener needs the side to distinguish this from the enemy row.

Trigger: The enemy gladiator's health reaches zero
Condition/Variant: HealthComponent.died on the enemy
Context: The opponent goes down and the player has won. Same animation as the hero's death but the framing is opposite - this one should feel earned rather than mourned.
SFX desc: Body fall with a defeated cry
VFX/Anim: Existing: 'death' animation
Notes: Fires immediately before battle_ended - order the two so the death lands first.

Trigger: A healing item is used on a gladiator
Condition/Variant: HealEffect.apply raises current health
Context: Meat goes in and the health bar jumps back up. The player just spent a scarce item and dragged it across the arena to do this, so the payoff needs to be immediate and clearly good.
SFX desc: Warm restorative swell with an eating or gulping element
VFX/Anim: Wanted: green flash or rising particles; nothing exists today
UI response: Health bar rises, nameplate numbers update
Notes: The heal itself works; there is no signal announcing it. HealthComponent.health_changed fires but cannot distinguish healing from damage.

Trigger: A healing item is used on a gladiator already at full health
Condition/Variant: current_health clamps at max, so nothing changes
Context: The player just burned an item for nothing. Better to tell them than to let the item silently vanish - a small negative cue teaches them to hold the meat until it matters.
SFX desc: Soft dull reject, clearly not the healing sound
UI response: Health bar visibly does not move
Notes: The clamp lives in HealthComponent.current_health's setter; detecting waste means comparing before and after.

Trigger: A gladiator pays stamina for an attack
Condition/Variant: Every beat, before the swing animation starts
Context: The steady drain that runs under every fight. On its own it is far too frequent to sound - this row exists so the stamina bar movement is catalogued, and so a designer can decide it stays silent.
SFX desc: Probably none; the bar movement carries it
UI response: Stamina bar ticks down on the nameplate
Notes: Fires on every single beat of the match. Do not attach a one-shot here without deciding it is not fatiguing.

Trigger: A gladiator's stamina reaches zero
Condition/Variant: has_stamina() flips false; the attack rhythm immediately halves
Context: The fighter is spent. Their whole attack pattern stretches to half speed from this instant, which is a large mechanical swing the player must notice - if they miss it they will not know to feed a potion.
SFX desc: Gasping exhale, sagging tone; a clear state-change sound
VFX/Anim: Wanted: slumped posture or sweat particles
Notes: _speed_scale is polled per frame and emits nothing; the crossing needs latching in StaminaComponent so it fires once, not every frame.

Trigger: Sustained bed while a gladiator is out of stamina
Condition/Variant: Runs for as long as has_stamina() is false
Context: The ongoing state, not the moment it began. Heavy breathing under the fight that keeps quietly telling the player their gladiator is struggling until they fix it.
SFX desc: Looping laboured breathing, low in the mix
UI response: Empty stamina bar for the duration
Notes: Needs both edges of the crossing to start and stop cleanly; a unit can die while exhausted, so death must also stop this.

Trigger: A stamina item is used on a gladiator
Condition/Variant: StaminaRestoreEffect.apply raises current stamina
Context: A potion goes in and the stamina bar refills. Distinct from the health item because the payoff is a change in fight tempo rather than survival - the player should hear the drink and then see the swings speed up.
SFX desc: Fizzing or gulping restore with an upward lift
VFX/Anim: Wanted: blue flash or bubbles
UI response: Stamina bar rises on the nameplate
Notes: Fires whether or not the fighter was actually exhausted.

Trigger: A gladiator that was out of stamina has some again
Condition/Variant: has_stamina() flips back to true after being false
Context: The fighter gets their wind back and the attack rhythm snaps to full speed. This is the reward beat for the potion the player just spent, and it is what stops the exhausted breathing loop.
SFX desc: Relieved breath and a recovery lift; also the stop cue for the exhausted loop
VFX/Anim: Wanted: posture straightens
Notes: Only fires on the false-to-true crossing - a top-up on a fighter who still had stamina is gladiator_stamina_restored, not this.

Trigger: A weapon item is dropped on a gladiator and worn
Condition/Variant: The item's EquipmentDef is a WeaponDef, RIGHT_HAND slot
Context: The player just armed their gladiator. The sword appears in the fighter's hand on the rig and every future swing hits for weapon damage. This is one of the most consequential things the player can do and should feel like a genuine upgrade.
SFX desc: Sword draw and grip, confident metallic ring
VFX/Anim: Existing: EquipmentRigSync sets sword_equipped on the rig
UI response: The dragged item disappears from the world
Notes: changed(slot) carries only the slot, not the kind - a listener must inspect the piece to tell weapon from armor. Only the hero has an EquipmentComponent.

Trigger: An armor item is dropped on a gladiator and worn
Condition/Variant: The item's EquipmentDef is an ArmorDef, e.g. the helmet in the HEAD slot
Context: The player just protected their gladiator. Incoming hits will now be soaked and the hit sound changes from flesh to metal, so this needs to sound protective and settled - a buckle, not a blade.
SFX desc: Leather and metal buckling on, settling weight
VFX/Anim: Existing: EquipmentRigSync sets helmet_equipped on the rig
UI response: The dragged item disappears from the world
Notes: CHEST and LEFT_HAND slots exist in the enum but have no art or defs yet.

Trigger: A new piece is equipped into an already-occupied slot
Condition/Variant: equip() returns a non-null previous piece
Context: Fresh gear replaces worn gear mid-fight. The old piece is not destroyed - it pops back out into the pile with its remaining durability intact - so this should read as a trade rather than a loss.
SFX desc: Layer a quick unbuckle or sheath under the equip sound
VFX/Anim: Existing: rig toggles stay on; the slot's contents change
Notes: Pairs with equipment_displaced_piece_popped, which fires on the same frame - do not double up two loud sounds.

Trigger: The replaced piece is thrown back into the arena
Condition/Variant: Only when a swap displaced something
Context: The old sword or helmet is lobbed up out of the gladiator's hands and tumbles back into the pile, still carrying its scars. The player can pick it up again, so it needs to visibly and audibly land rather than just appear.
SFX desc: Light gear toss; the landing is covered by item_landed
VFX/Anim: Existing: item spawns above the unit with upward velocity and spin
UI response: Durability pie on the popped piece reflects its remaining wear
Notes: Spawned into the item container, so it obeys the normal despawn timer from that moment.

Trigger: An equipped weapon loses a durability point
Condition/Variant: Every landed strike costs the weapon exactly 1
Context: The sword chips a little on every hit it lands. Individually trivial and far too frequent for a dedicated sound, but the wear is what makes weapons temporary, so it should be audible as texture inside the impact rather than as its own event.
SFX desc: Fold into the sword impact rather than adding a separate sample
UI response: Durability pie shrinks (visible only while hovered or dragged)
Notes: Damage is read before wear is charged, so the strike that empties a weapon still lands at full weapon damage.

Trigger: An equipped armor piece loses a durability point
Condition/Variant: One randomly chosen equipped armor piece per incoming hit
Context: Armor grinds down as it absorbs. Like weapon wear this is constant and belongs inside the hit sound as a scrape, not as its own cue - but it is why armor is a consumable and not a permanent upgrade.
SFX desc: Fold a scrape into the armored hit sounds
Notes: Every equipped armor piece contributes its full value to mitigation, but only one random piece takes the wear.

Trigger: An equipped piece drops to its last durability point
Condition/Variant: durability reaches 1 after a wear tick
Context: One more hit and this piece is gone. The player has a short window to swap in a replacement, and the durability pie is only visible on hover, so audio is the only way they find out in time.
SFX desc: Stressed creak or crack; ominous, not alarming
VFX/Anim: Wanted: the equipped piece could flash on the rig
UI response: Durability pie is nearly all red
Notes: The pie is hidden unless the item is hovered or dragged - see Item.set_durability_shown - which is exactly why this needs a sound.

Trigger: An equipped weapon's durability runs out and it is destroyed
Condition/Variant: broken(slot, item_def) for a WeaponDef
Context: The sword shatters mid-fight and simply vanishes from the gladiator's hand. The player's next swing will be a bare fist doing less damage. This is a small disaster and needs to cut through the crowd bed so it is never missed.
SFX desc: Sharp metal snap and shards; the most attention-grabbing gear sound
VFX/Anim: Existing: EquipmentRigSync clears sword_equipped - the sword disappears
Notes: Broken gear vanishes entirely - there is no world drop. broken and changed both fire; wire audio to broken only.

Trigger: An equipped armor piece's durability runs out and it is destroyed
Condition/Variant: broken(slot, item_def) for an ArmorDef
Context: The helmet gives way. From the very next hit the fighter is taking full damage again, and the hit sounds revert from metal to flesh. The player needs to hear this to know their protection is gone.
SFX desc: Crumpling metal collapse; heavy, final
VFX/Anim: Existing: EquipmentRigSync clears helmet_equipped
Notes: If it was the last armor piece, subsequent hits switch to gladiator_hit_unarmored.

Trigger: The gladiator's visible gear is synced to the equipment state
Condition/Variant: Any changed(slot) with a rig property mapped for that slot
Context: The purely visual half of equipping and breaking - the sword or helmet appearing or disappearing on the fighter's body. Included so the visual sync is catalogued alongside its audio, not because it needs its own sound.
SFX desc: None; the equip and break rows own the audio
VFX/Anim: Existing: sets sword_equipped / helmet_equipped on the GladiatorBones rig
Notes: Only HEAD and RIGHT_HAND are mapped in SLOT_PROPERTIES; other slots sync nothing.

Trigger: The player drops equipment on a gladiator that cannot wear it
Condition/Variant: can_use_on returns false - the enemy has no EquipmentComponent
Context: The player tried to put a helmet on the enemy. Nothing happens, the item stays in the pile, and right now they get no explanation at all - it just looks broken. A small reject cue turns a bug-feeling moment into a rule.
SFX desc: Short dull reject thunk
VFX/Anim: Existing: the drop-zone outline never lit, so there was no promise to break
Notes: The absent hover outline is already the negative feedback; decide whether a sound on release adds clarity or just noise.

Trigger: A spawner lobs a new item into the arena
Condition/Variant: Every spawn_interval (plus jitter) while spawning is enabled
Context: Items are thrown in from the sides on a constant timer - this is where the whole item economy comes from. It happens roughly once a second all match, so it must be light enough to live in the background.
SFX desc: Short whoosh of something thrown in from offscreen
VFX/Anim: Existing: item launches on an arc with random spin
Notes: Fires about once a second per spawner and there are two spawners - keep it quiet or drop it in favour of the landing sound.

Trigger: A thrown item hits the arena floor or a wall
Condition/Variant: First contact with the world layer
Context: The clatter of the pile growing. This is the sound the arena floor makes all match and is the main texture of the item layer, so it should vary by item type - a sword clangs, meat slaps, a potion clinks.
SFX desc: Per-type impact: metal, flesh, glass, junk
VFX/Anim: Existing: RigidBody2D physics settle
Notes: Item has no contact reporting today - contact_monitor and max_contacts_reported must be enabled, which is a per-body cost worth measuring against a pile of items.

Trigger: Two items knock into each other in the pile
Condition/Variant: Item-on-item contact above a velocity threshold
Context: Items jostling as the pile churns and as the player drags one through it. Very frequent and easy to turn into mush, so it needs a velocity gate and a hard limit on how many can sound at once.
SFX desc: Light clatter, quieter than a landing
VFX/Anim: Existing: physics only
Notes: Needs both a velocity threshold and a voice cap or the pile becomes a wall of noise.

Trigger: The player picks an item up with the mouse
Condition/Variant: Left mouse down over an item, including one sitting in a hotbar slot
Context: The player takes hold of something. The item becomes light and follows the cursor on a spring. This is the most-repeated player action in the game and the confirmation that the click landed on the item they meant.
SFX desc: Quick pick-up scuff, varied per item type
VFX/Anim: Existing: item switches to the light carried physics profile
UI response: Durability pie shows if the item is equipment
Notes: Also fires when lifting an item back out of a hotbar slot - the slot treats it as gone at that moment.

Trigger: Sustained bed while an item is being carried
Condition/Variant: For as long as a drag is live
Context: The item is dangling off the cursor and dragging through the pile on its way to the gladiator. A light continuous cue helps sell the weight of what is being carried.
SFX desc: Soft looping scrape or rustle, very low in the mix
VFX/Anim: Existing: spring force pulls the item toward the cursor
Notes: The dragged item can despawn out from under the drag - the loop must stop on that too, not just on release.

Trigger: The player lets go of a carried item
Condition/Variant: Left mouse up; covers drops that do nothing as well as ones that land somewhere
Context: The player lets go. What happens next depends on where the cursor is - a hotbar slot, a gladiator, or nothing at all - so this is the neutral release and the follow-up rows carry the outcome.
SFX desc: Light release, deliberately neutral
VFX/Anim: Existing: item returns to the heavy physics profile
Notes: Also the stop cue for item_dragged_loop. Hotbar placement is checked before any gladiator - UI wins over world.

Trigger: A carried item is dragged over a gladiator that can use it
Condition/Variant: Unit alive and can_use_on true; equipment additionally needs an EquipmentComponent
Context: The gladiator lights up under the dragged item - the game promising that releasing here will do something. This is the single most useful piece of feedback in the drag interaction and currently has no sound at all.
SFX desc: Soft rising hover tick; must not repeat while the hover is held
VFX/Anim: Existing: outline shader on the unit's sprite (ItemDropZone.set_highlighted)
Notes: _set_hover_zone already early-returns when the zone has not changed, so a sound wired here fires once per hover rather than per frame.

Trigger: The drag leaves a highlighted gladiator
Condition/Variant: Hover zone changes to null or to a different unit
Context: The promise is withdrawn - releasing here would now do nothing. Quieter than the enter cue; mostly it just needs to not be silent, so the player knows the state changed.
SFX desc: Soft falling counterpart to the hover tick
VFX/Anim: Existing: outline cleared
Notes: Also fires when the drag ends or the item despawns mid-drag.

Trigger: An item is released somewhere that does nothing with it
Condition/Variant: No hotbar slot and no valid drop zone under either the cursor or the item
Context: The player dropped an item on empty sand. Common and usually intentional - they are just clearing the pile - so this should be almost nothing, distinct from a genuine rejection.
SFX desc: Nothing beyond the physical landing
VFX/Anim: Existing: the item just falls
Notes: Distinct from equipment_reject_invalid_target, where the player aimed at a gladiator and was refused.

Trigger: Meat is used on a gladiator
Condition/Variant: ItemDef.Type.MEAT with a HealEffect
Context: The gladiator eats. The item is consumed and vanishes from the world, and the health bar jumps. Should sound like food, and should be immediately distinguishable from drinking a potion so the player can confirm they grabbed the right thing.
SFX desc: Tearing and chewing, wet and satisfying
VFX/Anim: Wanted: green heal flash
UI response: Health bar rises; the item is gone from the pile
Notes: Reachable from both routes: dropped on a gladiator, or spent from a hotbar slot with a number key.

Trigger: A potion is used on a gladiator
Condition/Variant: ItemDef.Type.POTION with a StaminaRestoreEffect
Context: The gladiator drinks. Same consumption path as meat but the payoff is tempo rather than survival, so it must sound clearly different - glass and liquid against the meat's tearing.
SFX desc: Cork, glug, glass set-down
VFX/Anim: Wanted: blue restore flash
UI response: Stamina bar rises; the item is gone from the pile
Notes: Same route as meat; the def's effect subclass is what differs.

Trigger: The durability pie appears over an item
Condition/Variant: The item is equipment and is hovered or dragged
Context: The little red-and-green wear gauge pops up over a piece of gear as the cursor passes it. Pure information, and it appears constantly as the cursor sweeps the pile - almost certainly silent.
SFX desc: None recommended
VFX/Anim: Existing: DurabilityPie becomes visible and redraws
UI response: The pie itself is the UI
Notes: Hidden by default so the pile is not a wall of gauges - see the DurabilityPie doc comment.

Trigger: An item starts blinking near the end of its lifetime
Condition/Variant: Despawn timer fires; the blink runs out the last few seconds of lifetime
Context: An item in the pile starts blinking: it has a few seconds left. Often the player is mid-reach for it. Should be a gentle nag, not an alarm - it fires constantly as the pile churns and must not become fatigue.
SFX desc: Very soft repeating tick, or nothing at all
VFX/Anim: Existing: alpha blinks down to blink_min_alpha and back, blink_rate times a second
UI response: A blinking item is still grabbable - alpha stays above 0 on purpose
Notes: With two spawners running this can be true for several items at once - needs a voice cap if it gets a sound.

Trigger: An item reaches the end of its lifetime and is freed
Condition/Variant: The blink tween finishes
Context: The item is gone. The warning blink already told the player it was coming, so this is a confirmation rather than news - and it is what keeps the pile from growing without bound.
SFX desc: Soft poof, or silence if the blink warning already carries it
VFX/Anim: Existing: the node is freed and disappears
UI response: Clears from a hotbar slot if it was parked in one
Notes: Can happen while the item is being dragged or is sitting in a hotbar slot - a slot preserves the item, not its lifetime.

Trigger: Junk is dropped on a gladiator
Condition/Variant: ItemDef.Type.JUNK - no effect and no equipment
Context: The player fed the gladiator a piece of rubbish. Junk is the filler that makes the pile something to sift through, so being told plainly that this piece does nothing is part of teaching the item mix.
SFX desc: Flat comedic nothing-sound
VFX/Anim: Existing: no outline highlight while hovering, since can_use_on is false
UI response: Nothing happens; the item stays in the world
Notes: Junk is drawn as the plain grey placeholder square and carries no texture.

Trigger: A released item is claimed by a hotbar slot
Condition/Variant: The cursor or the item overlaps an empty slot's rect on release
Context: The player banks an item for later. It snaps upright into the slot and freezes there, ready for a number key. Needs a positive, definite click so the player knows it was caught rather than dropped on the floor.
SFX desc: Crisp slot-click with a little heft
VFX/Anim: Existing: item freezes upright at the slot's world position
UI response: The item renders inside the slot square
Notes: The item stays a live RigidBody2D in the world behind the UI layer; its despawn countdown keeps running.

Trigger: An item is placed into a slot that already held one
Condition/Variant: The previous occupant is destroyed outright
Context: The player overwrites a stored item and the old one is gone for good - replaced, not swapped back into the pile. That is a real loss and should sound different from filling an empty slot.
SFX desc: Slot click with a discard element underneath
VFX/Anim: Existing: the old item is freed and vanishes
Notes: The displaced item is queue_free'd, not returned to the pile - unlike an equipment swap.

Trigger: A slotted item leaves its slot
Condition/Variant: Dragged out, consumed, or despawned in place
Context: The slot empties. Three different causes funnel here, so this is a neutral empty-out rather than a reaction - the grab and consume rows carry the flavour.
SFX desc: Soft slot-empty tick
VFX/Anim: Existing: item unfreezes and rejoins the pile, or is freed
Notes: Both exit routes are wired to the same handler - do not also play the grab sound here or it doubles.

Trigger: The player presses a hotbar number key with an item stored
Condition/Variant: hotbar_1 through hotbar_4, hero alive, slot occupied
Context: The player spends a banked item on their gladiator without dragging anything. This is the fast path in a tense moment - the health bar is low and they are hitting a key - so it needs instant confirmation.
SFX desc: Key-activate click; the consume sound follows immediately
VFX/Anim: Existing: the item is consumed and freed
UI response: Slot empties and the relevant bar rises
Notes: Layers with item_consumed_meat or _potion on the same frame - keep this one short.

Trigger: The player presses a number key for an empty slot
Condition/Variant: Slot is empty
Context: The player reached for something that is not there. Under pressure they will mash these keys, so telling them the slot is empty is more useful than silence - but it must stay quiet enough to survive being spammed.
SFX desc: Very short dull reject
VFX/Anim: Wanted: the empty slot could flash
Notes: Currently a silent no-op via HotbarSlot.use_on's is_empty() guard.

Trigger: The player presses a hotbar key after their gladiator has died
Condition/Variant: hero is null or not alive
Context: The match is already lost and the player is still hitting keys. Should read as unmistakably over - not the same reject as an empty slot, because no slot would work now.
SFX desc: Flat dead reject, lower than the empty-slot cue
Notes: Guarded at the top of _use_slot before any slot is consulted.

Trigger: The cursor moves over a menu button
Condition/Variant: Any focusable menu button
Context: Standard menu navigation feedback. Applies across the title screen, options and pause menu, so it must be inoffensive at high repetition.
SFX desc: Light navigate tick
VFX/Anim: Existing: theme hover state
Notes: Asset exists: common/audio/sfx/ui/aud_ui_navigate.wav

Trigger: The player clicks a menu button
Condition/Variant: Any menu button, including the battle restart button
Context: The universal confirm. Every menu action and the restart button route through here, so it is the most-heard sound in the game outside combat.
SFX desc: Standard UI confirm click
VFX/Anim: Existing: theme pressed state
Notes: Asset exists: common/audio/sfx/ui/UI_CLICK.wav. Note this same file is currently misused as the combat HIT_SOUND in auto_battle_prototype.gd - replacing that is the first job once combat SFX land.

Trigger: A menu panel opens
Condition/Variant: Options opened from the title or pause menu
Context: A panel slides in over whatever was there. Paired with the close cue so the menu feels like it has depth rather than just swapping screens.
SFX desc: Panel open whoosh
VFX/Anim: Existing: panel visibility swap
Notes: Asset exists: common/audio/sfx/ui/aud_ui_open.wav

Trigger: A menu panel closes
Condition/Variant: Back pressed from the options menu
Context: The reverse of the open cue - the player is stepping back out to where they were.
SFX desc: Panel close whoosh
VFX/Anim: Existing: panel visibility swap
Notes: Asset exists: common/audio/sfx/ui/aud_ui_close.wav

Trigger: The player pauses mid-match
Condition/Variant: The pause action is pressed while unpaused
Context: The arena freezes. The fight stops dead mid-swing and the pause panel appears, so the audio needs to clearly mark the world going quiet - ideally the ambient bed and music duck here too.
SFX desc: Pause thunk with a filter sweep on everything else
VFX/Anim: Existing: the whole tree pauses; the pause panel becomes visible
Music: Duck or pause the battle music
Notes: Asset exists: common/audio/sfx/ui/aud_ui_pause.wav. Zero-argument signal, so SoundEffectConnector can wire it as pure scene data.

Trigger: The player resumes the match
Condition/Variant: Pause toggled off, or Continue pressed
Context: The fight snaps back to life from exactly where it froze. Should feel like a release of held breath, and must restore whatever the pause ducked.
SFX desc: Release thunk, filter sweep back open
VFX/Anim: Existing: tree unpauses, panel hides
Notes: Asset exists: common/audio/sfx/ui/aud_ui_unpause.wav. Emitted from both pause() and unpause().

Trigger: The player drags a volume slider
Condition/Variant: Any of the five bus sliders in the options menu
Context: The player is setting volumes and needs to hear the result. The tick has to play on the bus being adjusted or it tells them nothing about that bus.
SFX desc: Short neutral tick
VFX/Anim: Existing: slider moves
UI response: Bus volume applied and written to the settings file
Notes: Routing matters: main_menu_audio.gd currently plays this on the UI player regardless of which bus was moved.

Trigger: The player switches between windowed and fullscreen
Condition/Variant: The window button is pressed
Context: The window resizes under the player. A confirmation is useful because the visual change is dramatic enough to be disorienting.
SFX desc: Standard UI confirm click
VFX/Anim: Existing: the OS window mode changes
Notes: Written back to user://settings.tres immediately.

Trigger: A fade-out begins before a scene change
Condition/Variant: change_scene_with_transition is called
Context: The screen fades to black on the way somewhere else. Covers title-to-match, match-to-menu and credits, and is the natural place to fade the outgoing music.
SFX desc: Low transition whoosh
VFX/Anim: Existing: the transition scene plays its FadeOut animation
Notes: Zero-argument signal on the SceneTransitionManager autoload, not on GlobalSignalBus.

Trigger: The fade-in completes on the new scene
Condition/Variant: The FadeIn animation ends and the transition frees itself
Context: The new scene is revealed and playable. This is where the incoming music and ambience should be up to full.
SFX desc: Transition tail
VFX/Anim: Existing: the transition instance frees itself
Notes: Zero-argument signal on the SceneTransitionManager autoload.

Trigger: The title screen becomes active
Condition/Variant: Emitted from the main menu's audio node on ready
Context: First thing the player ever hears. Sets the tone for the whole game before they have seen a single swing.
SFX desc: Optional title sting over the music
VFX/Anim: Existing: main menu shown
Notes: Zero-argument signal - wirable through SoundEffectConnector with no code.

Trigger: The credits screen is opened
Condition/Variant: Credits pressed on the main menu
Context: The player is reading who made this. Wants its own music bed and no gameplay noise underneath.
SFX desc: None; the music carries it
VFX/Anim: Existing: scene change with a fade
Notes: Zero-argument signal - wirable through SoundEffectConnector.

Trigger: The player quits the game
Condition/Variant: Quit pressed on the title or pause menu; hidden on web builds
Context: The application is about to close. Almost anything here is cut off by the process exiting, so treat it as optional.
SFX desc: Very short exit tone, if any
VFX/Anim: Existing: the window closes
Notes: get_tree().quit() returns immediately - a long sample will not finish.

Trigger: A floating damage number appears over a gladiator
Condition/Variant: Every unit_hurt emission
Context: The number that drifts up off a struck fighter, including a 0 when armor ate the hit. Purely visual and already implemented - listed so the feedback catalogue is complete and so it is not accidentally given a sound of its own on top of the impact.
SFX desc: None; the hit rows own the audio
VFX/Anim: Existing: DamageNumber drifts up 40px and fades over ~0.7s
Notes: This handler also currently plays UI_CLICK.wav as a placeholder hit sound - that call is what the combat hit rows replace.

Trigger: The arena crowd bed begins
Condition/Variant: On entering a battle scene
Context: The colosseum is full and never silent. This bed runs under the entire match and is what makes the arena feel occupied - every other sound is mixed against it, so it must sit low and leave room.
SFX desc: Looping crowd murmur bed
Notes: common/audio/sfx/cheerwhitenoise.ogg already exists and can serve as this bed. Route through GSoundManager.SoundPlayerType.AMBIENT.

Trigger: The arena crowd bed ends
Condition/Variant: On leaving the battle scene
Context: The crowd falls away as the player exits to a menu. Purely the stop half of the pair.
SFX desc: Fade out rather than a hard cut
Notes: Stop cue for ambient_arena_bed_start. GSoundManager.stop_sound(AMBIENT) already exists.

Trigger: The crowd reacts to a solid blow
Condition/Variant: Damage above a threshold; rate-limited so it cannot fire every swing
Context: The crowd surges at a big hit. This is what makes the arena feel alive and reactive rather than a looped murmur - but it must be gated, or a fast attack pattern turns it into constant noise.
SFX desc: Short crowd swell over the bed
Notes: Needs a damage threshold and a cooldown. A rogue-pattern unit can land several hits per second.

Trigger: The crowd reacts to the hero taking a heavy hit
Condition/Variant: Hero side, large damage relative to remaining health
Context: The crowd is on the player's side. A gasp when their fighter takes a bad one reinforces that the player has a stake in this, and doubles as a warning that health is getting dangerous.
SFX desc: Sharp intake, crowd-wide
Notes: hero_gladiator_hurt is a single-argument signal, so SoundEffectConnector can bind it directly - but the connector cannot apply a damage threshold.

Trigger: The crowd erupts when the hero wins
Condition/Variant: battle_ended(true)
Context: The payoff moment. Should be the loudest thing in the game and should carry over the top of the victory stinger rather than competing with it.
SFX desc: Full crowd roar with a long tail
Notes: Should replace or heavily duck the ambient bed for its duration.

Trigger: The crowd turns when the hero loses
Condition/Variant: battle_ended(false)
Context: The crowd is not on the player's side any more. A jeer or a disappointed fall-away, deliberately the opposite shape to the victory roar.
SFX desc: Jeering or a deflating fall-away
Notes: Same signal as the victory roar - the bool argument decides which.

Trigger: The crowd reacts to a gladiator's gear shattering
Condition/Variant: Any broken(slot, item_def)
Context: The crowd notices the sword go. A reaction here makes gear breaking feel like an event in the arena rather than a private inventory change, and reinforces a moment the player must not miss.
SFX desc: Short surprised crowd reaction
Notes: Layer under the break sound, offset slightly so the break reads first.

Trigger: The crowd bed shifts intensity as the match swings
Condition/Variant: Driven by combined health, or by how close the fight is
Context: The crowd gets louder as the fight gets closer. A continuous dynamic layer rather than an event, so the arena tracks the drama without anyone scripting it per match.
SFX desc: Cross-faded intensity layers over the base bed
Music: Should move together with the music intensity
Notes: Design sketch exists at docs/design/audio-systems/dyn-music.excalidraw.json.

Trigger: Title screen music starts
Condition/Variant: On entering the main menu
Context: The game's front door. Loops indefinitely while the player sits on the menu or pokes at options.
Notes: Track exists; nothing plays it today.

Trigger: Pre-fight music plays
Condition/Variant: During the match intro beat, before the fighting starts
Context: The walk-out. Sets the arena up before the first swing, then hands off to the battle track.
Notes: Depends on match_intro existing; today the fight starts on the first frame.

Trigger: The battle track's intro section plays
Condition/Variant: At match start
Context: The one-shot opening of the battle theme, which hands over to the loop when it ends. MusicPlayerLooper already implements exactly this intro-then-loop handoff.
Notes: The looper exists and is unused in the battle scene. It hands over on a timer sized from the intro's own length.

Trigger: The battle track's main loop takes over
Condition/Variant: When the intro section finishes
Context: The bed the whole fight happens over. Everything else - hits, crowd, gear breaking - is mixed against this, so it must leave headroom in the mid-range.
Notes: Also stopped by match_ended_hero_defeat; only one stop event fits this column.

Trigger: Victory music plays
Condition/Variant: battle_ended(true)
Context: Plays over the crowd roar and under the result panel. Should start the instant the enemy falls, not when the panel appears roughly a second later.
Notes: Cut the battle loop first so the two do not overlap.

Trigger: Defeat music plays
Condition/Variant: battle_ended(false)
Context: The counterpart to the victory track, on the same timing. The player is watching their gladiator fall and about to be offered a restart.
Notes: Same signal as victory - the bool argument decides which.

Trigger: The music ducks so a key moment can cut through
Condition/Variant: Gear breaking, a death, or any other stinger event
Context: Not a sound of its own - a mixing behaviour. The battle loop drops a few dB for a moment so a shattering sword or a killing blow is unmistakable instead of buried.
Music: Applies to whichever track is playing
Notes: Implement as a bus-level ducking on the Music bus; the buses already exist in default_bus_layout.tres.
